verdicts.title: AI ROI Pilot: vertical landing variants (transport/construction/care)
verdicts.author: ColaberryIntern
blockers: Merge conflict against main: mergeable=CONFLICTING / mergeStateStatus=DIRTY (stable across three reads). Branch workstream/ai-pilot-verticals cannot be merged until rebased onto latest main and conflicts resolved. Likely conflict points are the two high-churn shared files in the diff (PROGRESS.md, frontend/src/App.tsx), both routinely edited by concurrent sessions and the route table.
majors: Token-refresh write/read asymmetry: scripts/refreshBasecampTokenFromVault.sh updates a hardcoded row (BasecampAuthInfoID=237) while every CCPP reader selects the newest active row (SELECT TOP 1 ... WHERE IsActive=1 ORDER BY BasecampAuthInfoID DESC; directives/gov-bid-intake.md:31, backend/src/scripts/lib/basecampToken.js:27). If the rotation worker inserts a newer active row (documented in tmp/ccpp-kit/common-queries.md:170-172), the cron keeps refreshing stale row 237 while readers pick the newer un-refreshed row, silently reintroducing the biweekly 401 the script exists to prevent. Fix: target newest IsActive=1, matching the readers.

sendAiPilotOutreach.js ships safety-critical NEW logic with zero tests: suppression-file union (replied + suppression -> optedOut skip), write-before-send crash-safe idempotency reorder via atomic temp+rename with rollback on throw, and the /\d/ CAN-SPAM ADDRESS gate. Doctrine marks idempotency + opt-out NON-NEGOTIABLE and requires happy/failure/boundary/idempotency coverage per feature; none exist. The eligibility filter and writeSent are pure/file-I/O and directly unit-testable.

scripts/auditAiPilotCampaign.js (+116, new) contains untested bounce/complaint auto-suppression business logic that mutates ai-pilot-suppression.json, which directly gates real cold-email sends in sendAiPilotOutreach.js (line 126 reads the same file). The classification (badStates) + idempotent set-union + atomic rewrite are pure and trivially testable with a fixture array; no happy/failure/boundary/idempotency coverage exists. High blast radius: sender reputation + CAN-SPAM compliance.
nits: scripts/auditAiPilotCampaign.js: net-new outbound Mandrill (https.request) and health-probe (https.get) calls have no timeout, violating CLAUDE.md Security Enforcement and Failure-First Design (every outbound call needs an explicit 5-30s timeout). On a recurring cron (0 16,19,22 * * 1-5) a silent-but-open connection hangs indefinitely. Fix: req.setTimeout(15000, ...) + timeout option on https.get.

Two PROGRESS.md entries marked [x] whose own Verification line says the work was NOT executed (Basecamp refresher: 'NOT yet run on prod'; frontend variant cites a build-on-deploy rather than an observed pass), weakening the '[x] only with concrete evidence' HARD GATE.

New side-effecting /scripts logic ships with no automated test (suppression union-append, write-before-send rollback) — softer gate outside services/ but high-blast-radius per the Test Strategy framework.
AIPilotVerticalPage.tsx handleSubmit validation branch + lead-POST failure path have no component/render test (per-vertical slug attribution can silently regress).
sendAiPilotOutreach.js follow-up-touch gating (touch N>1 requires touch-1 history) and newestLeadsFile() multi-candidate selection lack boundary tests.

auditAiPilotCampaign.js interpolates Mandrill-derived state strings and AUDIT_DATE into the audit email HTML without escaping (low risk: single internal trusted recipient, enum-like values); add escapeHtml() as defense-in-depth.
ExpiresAt written as a relative duration (1209600) into a column named for an absolute timestamp — currently harmless (no reader consults ExpiresAt) but semantically misleading for a future refactor.
OUT_DIR defaults diverge (sender ./tmp vs auditor /app/tmp); aligned in the documented cron/run flow but a bare run without OUT_DIR set would point suppression read/write at different files.
verdicts.rationale: The merge facts decide this independently of code quality: GitHub reports mergeable=CONFLICTING / mergeStateStatus=DIRTY against main, stable across three reads, which is a verified hard merge stopper and forces BLOCK. On top of that, three verified majors stand: the token-refresh script writes a hardcoded row 237 while every reader selects the newest active row (a real idempotency defect under a documented rotation-insert path that would reintroduce the biweekly 401 it exists to prevent), and the two most safety-critical new code paths — suppression/idempotency/CAN-SPAM enforcement in sendAiPilotOutreach.js and the bounce auto-suppression in auditAiPilotCampaign.js that gates real sends — ship with zero tests despite the repo doctrine marking idempotency and opt-out enforcement NON-NEGOTIABLE. The positives are genuine (PROGRESS.md HARD GATE satisfied with six Session-ID-tagged entries, no committed secrets, parameterized MSSQL write, idempotent atomic writes, em-dash-clean email copy, clean React/lead-capture contract), but they do not clear the conflict or the untested-safety-logic gap. Note also self-approval-only: ColaberryIntern cannot approve their own PR, so even after the conflict and majors are addressed the gate needs Ali's admin merge or a second reviewer, and dismiss_stale_reviews=true means review must happen after the rebase.
verdicts.suggestedAction: Do not merge. Send back to the author: (1) rebase workstream/ai-pilot-verticals onto latest main and resolve the conflicts (expect PROGRESS.md and frontend/src/App.tsx); (2) fix refreshBasecampTokenFromVault.sh to target the newest IsActive=1 row instead of hardcoded id 237; (3) add unit tests for the suppression-union/write-before-send-rollback in sendAiPilotOutreach.js and the classification+set-union in auditAiPilotCampaign.js (happy/failure/boundary/idempotency), and add outbound timeouts to the Mandrill/health calls. After the rebase lands, this account cannot self-approve — Ali must admin-merge or route to a second reviewer, and the review must occur after the rebase since dismiss_stale_reviews=true will drop any pre-rebase approval.

verdicts.title: ops: wire Week 3 Anthropic Skilljar course link
verdicts.author: KesetebirhanDelele
blockers: PR has merge conflicts against main (mergeable=CONFLICTING / mergeStateStatus=DIRTY). Re-confirmed on re-poll and independently reproduced locally: `git merge-tree --write-tree origin/main origin/ops/wire-week3-skilljar-courses` exits 1 with CONFLICT (content) in PROGRESS.md. Main advanced past the branch's last main-merge (2026-06-19) and re-introduced the conflict on the shared append-only PROGRESS.md log. Branch must be re-synced with current main and the conflict resolved before it can merge.
majors: Backfilled Skilljar link is silently reverted on the next server restart. The PR edits seedCurriculum.ts (a standalone manual seed that is NEVER imported at boot), but the seeder wired into server startup is seedProgramCurriculum() (server.ts), which was left untouched. Its buildSessions() session_number=4 has no Skilljar entry, and its existing-session branch does an unconditional full materials_json overwrite on the oldest cohort every boot. So the next deploy/restart after running wireWeek3AnthropicCourses.js wipes the freshly-added link, and the PR's 'from day one' durability goal is unmet. Fix: add the same materials entry to buildSessions() session_number=4 in seedProgramCurriculum.ts and/or make the update branch merge rather than overwrite materials_json.

Week 3 PROGRESS.md entry is marked [x] with no concrete verification evidence, violating the CLAUDE.md HARD GATE ('No [x] mark without verification evidence on the same line; forbidden to mark complete based on intent'). The Verification line says `npx tsc --noEmit` is 'pending (background)' and substitutes 'structurally identical / pattern-validated' (intent) for a real artifact. Fix: run tsc and record the actual result, or revert the box to [ ] until evidence exists.

wireWeek3AnthropicCourses.ts ships DB-mutating dedup/merge logic with zero tests, against repo doctrine (idempotency NON-NEGOTIABLE; happy + failure + boundary + idempotency required for a shipped, prod-data-mutating backfill). Untested branches: Array.isArray guard over an any-typed materials_json (non-array input silently dropped), Set-based title dedup, prepend-merge ordering, empty-session early-exit. The repo already has the test pattern/infra (backend/src/__tests__/scripts/curriculumWeeks.test.ts). Logic is inlined in run(), so it is untestable as written. Fix: extract a pure mergeMaterials(existing, toAdd) helper and add ~5 unit cases.
nits: Stale base: head ca41904 dates 2026-06-19; main has diverged (9 commits ahead). Only PROGRESS.md actually conflicts; no curriculum/seed code files were touched on main since the merge base, so there is no code-level merge risk, but re-run tsc after re-sync since main touched neighboring backend files.
Scope-lock smell: PR also ships backend/src/scripts/addJuly2026Cohort.ts (a Cora cohort seed from a separate commit) that the PR title/body never mention. Disclose in the PR body or split.

Dev/prod-parity smell: wireWeek3AnthropicCourses.ts unconditionally loads .env.dev via dotenv even though its header says to run it in the prod container; the sibling addJuly2026Cohort.ts loads no dotenv. Make the load conditional or remove it.
PR notes/PROGRESS.md claim parity with wireWeek1/2AnthropicCourses.ts, which do not exist anywhere in the repo, so the 'pattern-validated' provenance is unsubstantiated.

Required approving review is missing (REVIEW_REQUIRED, no reviewers requested); dismiss_stale_reviews=true means any approval granted before the re-sync push will be dismissed, so approval must come AFTER the branch is updated.

Confirm curriculum mapping: the wire script targets session_number=4 ('Refinement & Executive Positioning'), while the Claude-API/agent-build content lives in session_number=2 ('Architecture & the 3-Agent System'). PR cites BC ticket #9984355649 as confirmation; flag for Ali's confirmation only.
verdicts.rationale: PR #48 is a small (4 files, 135 additions) data-seeding change and is clean on security (no secrets, no routes, parameterized Sequelize access). However it carries a hard merge stopper: GitHub reports mergeable=CONFLICTING / DIRTY, re-confirmed on re-poll and independently reproduced locally via git merge-tree (exit 1, content conflict in the append-only PROGRESS.md because main advanced past the branch's last 2026-06-19 main-merge). Per the verdict rules, a confirmed conflict forces BLOCK regardless of the code quality. On top of the conflict sit three verified majors: (1) the PR edits the wrong (non-startup) seeder, so the durable 'from day one' link is silently reverted by seedProgramCurriculum()'s unconditional materials_json overwrite on the next boot; (2) the Week 3 PROGRESS.md item is marked [x] with tsc only 'pending (background)', a direct violation of the enforced-now HARD GATE; and (3) the new DB-mutating backfill ships with zero tests despite the repo's non-negotiable idempotency/coverage doctrine and existing test precedent. None of these break security or the happy path, but all are real and actionable.
verdicts.suggestedAction: Do not merge. Send back to the author (KesetebirhanDelele): (1) re-sync the branch with current main and resolve the PROGRESS.md conflict; (2) add the Skilljar materials entry to buildSessions() session_number=4 in seedProgramCurriculum.ts (and/or make its update branch merge rather than overwrite materials_json) so the backfill survives restart; (3) run `npx tsc --noEmit` and record the real result in PROGRESS.md (or revert the [x] to [ ]); (4) extract a pure mergeMaterials() helper and add the ~5 unit tests. After the re-sync push, secure one approving review (dismiss_stale_reviews=true means approve only after the update), then Ali can admin-merge.

verdicts.rationale: PR #47 is a low-risk deploy slice: ~13 of 20 files are pure documentation/date-string edits, and the load-bearing changes (Cohort 1 kickoff 7/13->7/27 in curriculumWeeks.js with a matching test update, a 25->40 seat-cap SQL edit, and a new 197-line DRAFT Open House campaign seed wired into seedAllCampaigns.ts plus one non-secret env URL) are correct, idempotent, secret-free, and cannot send anything until a human activates. Merge mechanics are clean (mergeable=MERGEABLE, base=main, not draft, single squashed commit; BLOCKED is solely the required-approving-review gate, no CI configured). The one verified major is governance: CLAUDE.md's HARD GATE requires every commit touching /backend to also touch PROGRESS.md with a Session-ID-tagged entry and Verification line. Confirmed 7 backend files are changed (including the new seed) yet PROGRESS.md is absent from all 20 changed files, and the commit body itself states 'PROGRESS.md excluded,' making this a deliberate, confirmed Definition-of-Done violation. The seed's missing idempotency test is a real but minor gap: the find-or-create guard is actually implemented, blast radius is near-zero (DRAFT-only), and it matches repo precedent where the entire backend/src/seeds/ tree is untested and CLAUDE.md grandfathers it. No blockers and no merge-stopping conflicts exist, so the verdict is REQUEST_CHANGES, not BLOCK.
verdicts.suggestedAction: Ask the author to add a Session-ID-tagged PROGRESS.md entry for this change (e.g. Session CC-YYYYMMDD-xxxx, Verification: jest curriculumWeeks 17/17 + tsc --noEmit clean) before merge. Because the PR is self-approval-only (author=ColaberryIntern, GitHub blocks self-approval), once PROGRESS.md is added Ali must either admin-merge or approve from a distinct second account to clear the REVIEW_REQUIRED gate. Optionally request a one-line idempotency happy-path test for seedOpenHouseCampaigns.ts (asserting created===0 and same sequence_id on a second run), but that is a nit and not merge-blocking.

verdicts.title: ops: wire Week 2 Anthropic Skilljar course link
verdicts.author: KesetebirhanDelele
verdicts.rationale: PR #45 is a small, low-blast-radius ops change (4 files, +136/-0): two one-off backend scripts (addJuly2026Cohort.ts, wireWeek2AnthropicCourses.ts), a one-line seedCurriculum.ts addition wiring a public Anthropic Skilljar course link into Session 3 materials, and a PROGRESS.md entry. The code itself is clean across every dimension — correct against the Cohort/LiveSession schemas, genuinely idempotent (findOrCreate-by-name and title-Set dedup with explicit SKIP paths), no secrets, no injection surface, no new HTTP routes, PROGRESS.md updated with Session-ID-tagged entries, and the em-dash gate is not violated (em-dashes confined to internal prose/comments/logs). However, the PR carries a hard merge stopper: GitHub reports mergeable=CONFLICTING / mergeStateStatus=DIRTY, confirmed against live state on re-check (mergeable=CONFLICTING, mergeStateStatus=DIRTY, OPEN, base=main, not draft). I independently confirmed ground truth with a real three-way merge (git merge-tree --write-tree origin/main origin/ops/wire-week2-skilljar-courses), which produces exactly one conflict: CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in PROGRESS.md — the high-churn file appended by concurrent sessions. The branch already has a prior 'Merge branch main' reconciliation, but main has advanced and re-introduced the conflict. Per the verdict rules, a verified blocker AND a merge fact showing mergeable=CONFLICTING both force BLOCK. All other findings are minor/info (untested idempotency claims on disposable backend/src/scripts/ files, addJuly2026Cohort bundled in as an unrelated workstream, env-loading inconsistency, deferred-tsc verification) and do not independently move the verdict.
verdicts.suggestedAction: Do not merge yet. Ask the author to merge/rebase latest origin/main into ops/wire-week2-skilljar-courses and resolve the PROGRESS.md conflict by APPENDING both sessions' entries (never overwrite another Session ID — CLAUDE.md concurrent-instance safety), then push. Because dismiss_stale_reviews=true, resolve the conflict FIRST, then have a maintainer other than the author approve (author is KesetebirhanDelele, so the self-approval block does not apply; Ali can also admin-merge after one approval). No CI is configured, so empty status checks are expected and not a blocker.
blockers: Merge conflict against main: gh reports mergeable=CONFLICTING / mergeStateStatus=DIRTY (confirmed live), and an independent three-way merge (git merge-tree origin/main origin/ops/wire-week2-skilljar-courses) yields CONFLICT (content) in PROGRESS.md. PR cannot be merged until rebased/resolved.
nits: wireWeek2AnthropicCourses.ts asserts idempotency (title-Set dedup + prepend) but ships no test reproducing the second-run no-op; idempotency is a NON-NEGOTIABLE repo invariant though scripts are the 'disposable but auditable' tier.

wireWeek2AnthropicCourses.ts has no per-session error isolation (single top-level run().catch); a mid-batch session.update() failure leaves a partial commit, which Failure-First Design discourages — at minimum worth a doc note.
addJuly2026Cohort.ts is an unrelated July-cohort workstream (different Session ID CC-20260617-m9b3) bundled into a Skilljar-link PR (CC-20260618-w2sk); arguably should ship separately.

Env-loading inconsistency: wireWeek2 preloads .env.dev via dotenv while addJuly2026Cohort loads no dotenv; harmless on prod (DATABASE_URL present in container env) but the two scripts should share one convention.
PROGRESS.md marks the wire task [x] while its own Verification line says 'npx tsc --noEmit — pending VPS deploy'; verification is intent-adjacent rather than artifact-backed for a one-off script.
addJuly2026Cohort omits the sequelize.sync() that the cited seedCohorts.ts pattern performs — fine for prod (table exists) but would fail against a fresh empty DB.

verdicts.title: docs: re-confirm native student community (launch-min) — BC#9985688801
verdicts.author: ColaberryIntern
blockers: Merge conflict against main: mergeable=CONFLICTING / mergeStateStatus=DIRTY. Independently confirmed two ways — GitHub's settled (non-lazy) computation and a local `git merge-tree --write-tree origin/main e1b8c71` simulation that produces CONFLICT (content) in PROGRESS.md with all three merge stages populated. Main advanced past the PR's merge-base and multiple intervening commits appended to PROGRESS.md, colliding with PR #44's own tail append. Must rebase/merge onto current main and resolve the PROGRESS.md conflict, then re-push, before this PR can land.
verdicts.rationale: PR #44 is a clean docs-only decision record (3 Markdown files, +13/-2, 2 commits) re-confirming the student community as NATIVE at launch-min scope and deferring websocket presence + peer chat to P2. Across correctness, governance, tests, and security the diff is clean: no executable code, routes, SQL, secrets, or external calls; the PROGRESS.md HARD GATE isn't even triggered (no /backend /frontend /scripts /nginx /directives touched) yet the author still added a well-formed Session-ID-tagged entry with a concrete verification line; cross-file claims (launch-min scope, P2 deferral, Basecamp IDs 9985688801/9985688722) are internally consistent. The sole actionable issue is a hard merge stopper, verified two independent ways (GitHub settled state plus a local merge-tree simulation producing a real content conflict in PROGRESS.md). Per the verdict rules, a confirmed merge conflict forces BLOCK regardless of how clean the content is.
verdicts.suggestedAction: Do not merge yet. Ask the author (or resolve directly) to rebase/merge branch decision/community-native-reconfirm onto current origin/main and resolve the PROGRESS.md append conflict, then force-push. After the conflict-resolving push, because this is self-approval-only (author ColaberryIntern, GitHub blocks self-approval, dismiss_stale_reviews=true), the PR will need either a second reviewer's approval or Ali's admin-merge — and any earlier approval would be dismissed by the force-push, so it must be re-issued on the final commit. Once rebased clean, content is safe to admin-merge.

verdicts.title: ops: wire Week 1 Anthropic Skilljar course links for enterprise portal
verdicts.author: KesetebirhanDelele
verdicts.rationale: PR #43 is mechanically merge-ready (mergeable=MERGEABLE, base=main, not draft, no conflict; BLOCKED is only because main requires 1 approving review and none exists — there are no CI gates and empty statusCheckRollup is normal for this repo). The author is KesetebirhanDelele, a separate account from ColaberryIntern, so GitHub's self-approval block does not apply. The diff is small and clean on the merge, correctness, governance, and security dimensions: the production-facing change is a genuine two-part bug fix (React hooks-order violation + NaN-countdown parse fix) that was verified against real seeded data, PROGRESS.md's hard gate is satisfied with two Session-ID-tagged Verification entries, both new scripts are idempotent, and no secrets/routes/oversize-files are introduced. However, two independently adversarially-verified MAJOR findings on the tests dimension stand: (1) the new inline AM/PM-to-24h time parser — the entire reason for the fix — ships with zero regression or boundary tests despite an established repo pattern of extracting pure frontend logic into tested utils (projectPreviewUrl, promptTemplateUtils), violating the BUILD-BREAK-HARDEN rule that each fix lands with a test reproducing the original break; and (2) wireWeek1AnthropicCourses.ts is a DB-writing backfill that claims idempotency in comments but proves none, against a repo whose NON-NEGOTIABLE idempotency gate mandates a run-twice test and whose own convention in this very PR (dependencyLinks.test.ts, curriculumWeeks.test.ts, plus other lib+test pairs) is to extract pure decision logic into lib/ and unit-test it. Per the verdict rules, verified majors with no blockers and a clean merge state map to REQUEST_CHANGES. Confidence is moderate because both majors sit at the major/minor boundary — the strict "minimum now" unit-test gate is scoped to backend services/intelligence (frontend's stated gate is tsc --noEmit, and scripts/ is carved out as disposable), so these are BREAK-HARDEN process/quality gaps on a NON-NEGOTIABLE invariant and a production-facing student page rather than hard frontend gate breaches.
majors: New AM/PM-to-24h time parser (the NaN-countdown fix) in frontend/src/pages/portal/PortalSessionDetailPage.tsx ships with zero tests and no boundary coverage; extract to a frontend util (e.g. parseSessionTimeToHHMM) and add a jest test covering 12 PM stays 12, 12 AM becomes 00, 24-hour passthrough, and the malformed/null path (the original break), matching the repo's existing projectPreviewUrl/promptTemplateUtils util-test precedent and the BUILD-BREAK-HARDEN rule.

New side-effecting backfill script backend/src/scripts/wireWeek1AnthropicCourses.ts (writes LiveSession.materials_json across all session_number=2 rows) claims idempotency but has no idempotency/boundary test; extract the 'compute toAdd given existing materials' decision into a pure lib helper and test (a) first run prepends both, (b) second run adds nothing (idempotency), (c) partial-present adds only the missing one, (d) non-array/empty materials_json boundary — same lib+test pattern the rest of this PR already follows.
nits: Frontend hooks fix adds several un-justified `any` casts ((session as any) || {}, materials: any[], curriculum: any[]); CLAUDE.md bans `any` without a written justification comment on added lines — add a one-line justification or type the session shape.

seedAnthropicContentRegistry.ts now lists 5 confirmed Skilljar courses but anthropicContentWatcher.test.ts never asserts the confirmed-course count or that claude-code-101 is registered; optionally add one assertion pinning the 5 confirmed course URLs to guard against a future drop/typo.
One PROGRESS.md [x] entry rests on manual evidence ('hooks error gone in local CRA dev server') rather than a test name or deploy URL; acceptable but soft.

seedLocalDev.ts writes fake paid/active enrollments with year-long valid portal_token login URLs and has no NODE_ENV!=='production' guard; given the repo's documented history of dev instances pointing at the prod DB, add an explicit `if (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production') throw` before connect/sync.
seedLocalDev.ts core_time literal uses an en-dash (U+2013) — not the banned em-dash and not outbound content, so no gate violation; normalize to a hyphen for consistency if desired.
verdicts.suggestedAction: Do not approve yet. Ask KesetebirhanDelele to address the two test majors: (1) extract the AM/PM time parser to a frontend util and add a jest regression test (12 PM, 12 AM, 24h, malformed/null), and (2) extract the backfill's compute-toAdd decision into a pure lib helper and add an idempotency test (first run, second run no-op, partial, empty). Once those land (and ideally the `any`-justification nit), a single approving review from ColaberryIntern or any authorized reviewer merges it normally — no admin bypass needed since the author is a separate account.

verdicts.title: ops: add July 2026 cohort seed script (Cora shadow test case 7)
verdicts.author: KesetebirhanDelele
verdicts.rationale: PR #41 is a clean, low-blast-radius add-only change (+52/-0, 2 files, 1 commit): a 45-line idempotent Sequelize seed script (backend/src/scripts/addJuly2026Cohort.ts) inserting one "Cohort - July 2026" row via findOrCreate keyed on name, plus a compliant PROGRESS.md entry. No verified blockers or majors exist across any dimension. Correctness: every field used in defaults (name, start_date, core_day, core_time, optional_lab_day, max_seats, seats_taken, status) verifiably exists and is correctly typed in backend/src/models/Cohort.ts, status: 'open' as const matches the enum, all imports resolve, and 2026-07-23 is genuinely a Thursday matching core_day. Governance: the PROGRESS.md HARD GATE is satisfied (same diff touches /backend and adds a Session-ID-tagged entry CC-20260617-m9b3 with Date, What-changed, and Verification lines); idempotency, failure-path (.catch -> log + non-zero exit), no-secrets, and no-new-route gates all pass. Security: only compile-time constants flow through the parameterized ORM, no untrusted input, no secrets, no routes, no outbound HTTP, no PII. Merge facts are clean: mergeable=MERGEABLE, base=main, not draft, no conflicts. The only thing holding the merge is the required-approval branch-protection gate (reviewDecision=REVIEW_REQUIRED, mergeStateStatus=BLOCKED); empty statusCheckRollup is expected (no CI configured here). Author is KesetebirhanDelele, not ColaberryIntern, so the self-approval block does not apply. Remaining notes are purely informational/integration (e.g., the end-to-end "Cora returns July 23" outcome depends on Cora selection logic outside this diff; Verification line is narrower than a full clean tsc) and are not actionable defects.
verdicts.suggestedAction: Safe to approve and merge. A single approving review from any reviewer unblocks it (the only gate is branch-protection's 1-required-approval rule); Ali can also admin-merge. Author is not ColaberryIntern, so the self-approval restriction does not apply.
nits: Verification line in PROGRESS.md reads 'npx tsc --noEmit — no new errors on this file' rather than asserting a full clean project tsc; a full clean-tsc assertion would be stronger evidence (acceptable for a one-off seed script).

Script omits the sequelize.sync() that the reference seedCohorts.ts calls — correct/safer for the documented prod docker-exec path, but the alternate local-run instruction (ts-node against a fresh un-migrated DB) assumes the cohorts table already exists.

PR test plan is a manual runtime checklist (tsc only); no automated test, which is acceptable since this is a disposable one-off seed script under backend/src/scripts (mirrors the untested seedCohorts.ts).

verdicts.title: feat(portal): per-week Skilljar CourseLink — gates Epic 3
verdicts.author: ColaberryIntern
verdicts.rationale: PR #37 is a well-built, security-clean, and unusually well-tested feature (new curriculum_course_links table + fail-soft join service + idempotent seed + operator script + portal CTA, plus an unrelated pair of launch-PMO website KPI tiles), but it cannot merge. GitHub reports mergeable=CONFLICTING / mergeStateStatus=DIRTY (confirmed live and stable across calls), and an independent git merge-tree of origin/main against the head branch reproduces real content conflicts in exactly two files: PROGRESS.md (high-churn, expected) and backend/src/models/index.ts — a genuine source conflict in the Sequelize model registry where the branch adds CurriculumCourseLink while main independently added new model imports/associations (StudentGithubActivity, AiEvent, MentorReviewItem, LiveSession/MiniSection links). A careless resolution could silently drop models main added. The root cause is severe base drift: the branch is 67 commits behind main and only 2 ahead (merge-base e556c7f). Two verified blockers force BLOCK regardless of code quality. There is also a verified governance major: the PROGRESS.md entry (Session CC-20260617-r3m8) is marked [x] complete while its own Verification line admits tsc/jest were never run on the Windows host — a violation of the enforced PROGRESS.md hard gate. Remaining findings are minor/info (untested curriculumService join, untested seed/update idempotency paths, over-ceiling curriculumService.ts touched without split). Separately, because the author is ColaberryIntern, GitHub will block self-approval even after conflicts are resolved.
blockers: PR is CONFLICTING / DIRTY — GitHub will refuse the merge against main (confirmed live: mergeable=CONFLICTING, mergeStateStatus=DIRTY).

Concrete content conflicts in 2 files reproduced by git merge-tree: PROGRESS.md (high-churn changelog) and backend/src/models/index.ts (real model-registry conflict — branch adds CurriculumCourseLink, main added other model imports/associations; resolution must not drop main's models).
majors: Branch is 67 commits behind main and only 2 ahead (merge-base e556c7f) — severe stale base is the root cause of the conflict; the diff was written against an old tree and the test gate must be re-run after rebase since main moved PR-touched files (models/index.ts, curriculumService.ts, launchPmoDashboardHtml.js/.test.ts).

PROGRESS.md entry (CC-20260617-r3m8) is marked [x] complete but the Verification line admits node/tsc/jest were never run on the Windows host — violates the enforced PROGRESS.md hard gate requiring concrete verification evidence on the same line as an [x].
nits: curriculumService.ts course_link join (getParticipantCurriculum + getModulesForCohort) is new business logic in services/ with zero direct test coverage — add a focused unit test mocking getCourseLinkMap to assert matched->link, unmatched->null, empty-map->all null.

seedCurriculumCourseLinks() idempotent upsert (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE) is claimed idempotent in prose but never exercised by a test, despite idempotency being a NON-NEGOTIABLE in CLAUDE.md.
updateCourseLink run()/selectRow() — UPDATE-by-module path and the missing-row error throw are untested (only parseArgs/validateUpdate are covered).
curriculumService.ts (924->941 lines) was modified without the required split of an over-ceiling file (Modular Composition split-on-touch rule).

CourseLinkCta React component's three render branches have no unit test (acceptable under repo UI policy where tsc --noEmit is the minimum gate, but a RTL test would close the no-dead-link guarantee).
verdicts.suggestedAction: Do NOT merge. Ask the author to rebase/merge origin/main into feature/skilljar-courselink and resolve both conflicts — carefully for backend/src/models/index.ts so the new CurriculumCourseLink registration is added without dropping the model imports/associations main added (StudentGithubActivity, AiEvent, MentorReviewItem, LiveSession/MiniSection) — then re-push. Before re-review, have them actually run the gate (cd backend; npx tsc --noEmit; npx jest courseLinkService seedCurriculumCourseLinks updateCourseLink, and cd frontend; npx tsc --noEmit) and paste results into the PROGRESS.md Verification line. After conflicts clear, this still needs a second reviewer's approval or Ali's admin merge, since the ColaberryIntern author cannot self-approve.

verdicts.title: feat: enrollment tracking system — Stripe webhook + enrollment_leads [CC-20260616-s7r1]
verdicts.author: KesetebirhanDelele
blockers: Merge hard-blocked: GitHub reports mergeable=CONFLICTING / mergeStateStatus=DIRTY (re-confirmed live). Head 527a867c is 45 commits behind origin/main; a trial merge produces exactly one real content conflict in PROGRESS.md (backend/src/models/index.ts auto-merges cleanly). Branch must be rebased/merged onto current main and the PROGRESS.md conflict hand-resolved before it can land.

Cohort resolved by a non-existent cohort_number column — throws on the documented happy path. stripeWebhookService.ts:119 runs (Cohort as any).findOne({ where: { cohort_number: cohortNumber } }); the Cohort model and cohorts table have no cohort_number column (the (Cohort as any) cast hides the type error). When Stripe metadata carries the documented `cohort` key, Sequelize emits WHERE "cohort_number" = N, Postgres raises 42703, findOne THROWS (does not return null), so the if(!cohort) open-cohort fallback never runs. The exception 400s the webhook, Stripe retries identically forever: zero enrollment row, zero EnrollmentLead, zero seat increment, zero automation on the core paid-conversion path. Fix: query by a real cohort identifier (e.g. name) and/or seed a real cohort_number column, and wrap in try/catch so a bad lookup degrades to the open-cohort fallback.
majors: seats_taken incremented on enroll but never decremented on refund/withdrawal. handleChargeRefunded sets status='withdrawn'/payment_status='failed' and churns the lead but does not decrement Cohort.seats_taken, so the counter drifts upward monotonically. seats_taken is load-bearing: enrollmentService.ts gates enrollment on seats_taken >= max_seats (hard 400 'Cohort is full') and 4 other sites compute seats_remaining for availability/admin stats. A refunded student occupies a seat forever and can eventually false-block real enrollment. Fix: decrement seats_taken for the enrollment's cohort_id in handleChargeRefunded, guarded so it only fires once (e.g. only when the enrollment was not already withdrawn).

Idempotency guard is a check-then-act race, not transactional. handleCheckoutCompleted does Enrollment.findOne(stripe_payment_intent_id) then Enrollment.create with no try/catch on the unique constraint. Two concurrent Stripe re-deliveries can both pass findOne, and the second create throws SequelizeUniqueConstraintError, which propagates to the route's 400 (not a graceful 'already processed' skip), triggering further Stripe retries. The unique constraint prevents duplicate rows and double seat-increment (so no data corruption), but a replay surfaces as a spurious 400 + error log, violating the CLAUDE.md NON-NEGOTIABLE webhook pattern (unique constraint + ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING). Fix: catch the unique-violation as the duplicate-skip path, or use upsert/ON CONFLICT.

New side-effecting service ships with zero tests (Testing gate). stripeWebhookService.ts (+243) and the POST /api/webhook/stripe route handle financial/enrollment side effects (enrollment creation, seat increment, lead upsert, refund/withdraw, 3-way automation fan-out, HMAC signature verification + 400 error path) but the PR adds no *.test.ts (9 changed files, none are tests; none exist on the branch either). CLAUDE.md mandates every new services/ business-logic file ship with at least a happy-path unit test, and the analogous PaySimple webhook is already tested in two files (paysimpleService.test.ts, webhookController.test.ts) — established, met-elsewhere bar. At minimum: happy-path checkout (enrollment + lead + seat increment), idempotency guard (duplicate payment_intent skipped), and the signature-verification/invalid-signature 400 branch must be covered for Definition of Done.
nits: PROGRESS.md entry is marked [x] while its own Verification line admits the migration SQL was not yet run on prod at mark time — the runtime code depends on 8 new columns + enrollment_leads table that are unverified-applied. Split the migration-apply into its own tracked checkbox or note it as not-yet-deployed rather than a clean [x].

Unjustified `any` usages violate Contract Enforcement (no `any` without a written justification comment): two (Cohort as any).findOne casts in stripeWebhookService.ts (the very casts that hid the cohort_number type error) and catch (err: any) in webhookRoutes.ts. Type these properly or add one-line justification comments.

constructStripeEvent instantiates a full Stripe client via getStripeClient() (throws if STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is unset) just to verify a signature that only needs STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET — a partial misconfig 400s every webhook with a misleading 'STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is not configured' error. Use StripeLib.webhooks or build the client only when a network call is actually needed.

Stripe client built with no explicit timeout/maxNetworkRetries; harmless now (constructEvent is local crypto, no network I/O) but any future network call in this service inherits the 80s SDK default and 0 retries, against Failure-First Design. Set timeout + capped retries when adding the client.

Webhook catch returns raw err.message in the HTTP response body; post-verification DB errors could leak internal detail (constraint names). Low blast radius (only reachable by a validly-signed Stripe caller) but defense-in-depth says return a generic message and keep detail in the structured server log.
Customer email logged in cleartext (PII) in the stripe_enrollment_created log context; consider hashing or keying on enrollment_id alone if log access is broad.
upsertEnrollmentLead's re-enroll branch (findOrCreate created=false, status reset to 'enrolled', conditional phone backfill) is a distinct idempotency invariant with no test coverage.
verdicts.rationale: PR #28 is hard-blocked on two independently re-verified blockers. (1) Merge: live `gh pr view` returns mergeable=CONFLICTING / mergeStateStatus=DIRTY, and a local `git merge-tree origin/main 527a867c` produces exactly one real content conflict in PROGRESS.md (backend/src/models/index.ts auto-merges cleanly); the head is 45 commits behind main, so a clean merge is literally impossible until the branch is rebased and PROGRESS.md is hand-resolved. (2) Correctness: I confirmed stripeWebhookService.ts:119 queries a cohort_number column that does not exist in the Cohort model — on the documented happy path (Stripe metadata carries `cohort`) the findOne throws a Postgres 42703 rather than returning null, so the open-cohort fallback never runs, the webhook 400s, Stripe retries forever, and no enrollment/lead/seat/automation is ever created. That single defect silently breaks the core paid-conversion path. On top of these, three verified majors stand: seats_taken inflates on refunds (drifts a capacity gate that hard-blocks real enrollment), the idempotency guard is a non-transactional check-then-act race that surfaces replays as 400s (violating the NON-NEGOTIABLE webhook idempotency doctrine, though the unique constraint prevents data corruption), and the entire money-handling service + signature-verification route ship with zero tests against a met-elsewhere PaySimple precedent. Verdict is BLOCK on the verified blockers; the majors and nits are the remediation list once the blockers clear.
verdicts.suggestedAction: Do NOT merge. Send back to author (KesetebirhanDelele): (1) fix the cohort_number bug — resolve the cohort by a real identifier (e.g. Cohort name) and wrap the lookup in try/catch so a bad lookup degrades to the open-cohort fallback instead of 400ing; (2) decrement seats_taken in handleChargeRefunded (guarded to fire once); (3) make the idempotency guard replay-safe (catch unique-violation as a no-op or use upsert/ON CONFLICT); (4) add the missing happy-path + idempotency + invalid-signature tests; (5) rebase onto current main and hand-resolve the PROGRESS.md conflict. Then re-review. Author is KesetebirhanDelele (not ColaberryIntern), so the self-approval block does not apply — once fixed and rebased, ColaberryIntern or any write-access non-author can supply the one required approving review (collect it AFTER the rebase push, since dismiss_stale_reviews=true will void an earlier approval).
